title: Halloween in the Palace of Splendor
excerpt: Five weeks after Nevermind, the most important band in the world played their biggest show yet — for ten dollars a ticket, in their own city, in a room built for silent films.

title: A Brief Lightning Bolt
excerpt: Four musicians trying to save each other's lives made one album, played a handful of shows, and gave Seattle one of its most haunting nights.

title: The Godfather Comes to Capitol Hill
excerpt: Four days in a Seattle studio, one borrowed band, and Neil Young walked out with one of the best albums of his career — then played it for ninety people in a coffee shop.

title: Before the Box
excerpt: January 1990 in Pioneer Square, four guys from the local scene played the oldest saloon in Seattle before anybody outside the room knew their name.

title: The Night Before Everything Changed
excerpt: Three days before Nevermind was released, Nirvana played a punk club in Montréal for a crowd that had no idea what was about to happen.

title: Without Jerry
excerpt: Less than a year after Garcia died, the surviving Dead family brought a travelling festival to a minor-league ballpark in Maine to find out what they had left.

title: The Girl Who Came Home
excerpt: A week after Jagged Little Pill was released, Alanis Morissette stood on a tiny stage in her hometown and sang the songs that were about to swallow the world.

title: Two Worlds Collide on Halloween
excerpt: Dylan and a core member of the Grateful Dead shared a stage in Chicago on Halloween night, and neither of them was quite who the crowd remembered.

title: Four Minutes and Fifteen Seconds
excerpt: Seventeen songs into a show at the Troubadour, The Walkmen finally played "The Rat" — the encore the room had been quietly bracing for all night.

title: We Can Be Heroes
excerpt: A song written in the shadow of the Berlin Wall, sung by a man who had gone to Berlin to save his own life, played for twenty thousand people in London.

title: Dressed to Thrill
excerpt: Years after Urge Overkill had quietly fallen apart, two Chicago guys in matching suits walked onto a small London stage to see if any of it was still there.

title: The Longest Song in the World
excerpt: Iron Maiden played in the first week of the new Coliseum at Lansdowne Park, and a teenager from Ottawa stood in the back row holding his breath.

title: The Mystic and the King
excerpt: In the oldest Black neighbourhood in America, next to Congo Square, in a building that had held Louis Armstrong and Mahalia Jackson, two men arrived from opposite pressures and found the same door marked joy.

title: New Year's Eve at the Home of the Blues
excerpt: Stevie Ray Vaughan had been gone four months and four days, and four musicians he had taught or played with took the stage at the club where he learned to play.

title: Raw Power at the House of Piaf
excerpt: Three weeks after his only Top 40 hit in twenty-two years, Iggy Pop walked onto the stage where Édith Piaf had saved herself from bankruptcy — and played "Raw Power" for a generation about to inherit the world.
